_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of criticality and vulnerability factors (e.g., value, recuperation, cushion, countermeasures) for target selection and applicability to the cyber domain.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of AI risk taxonomy, including model risk, algorithmic bias, opacity, adversarial vulnerability, data poisoning, training data integrity failures, and systemic AI failure modes.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI AG domain. AG-K-003 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: SP-K-001 (SP, cosine: 0.500), SP-K-012 (SP, cosine: 0.491).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 4.5/10 (raw 0.455). Shared vocabulary: [knowledge, vulnerability]. DCWF-only terms [applicability, countermeasures, criticality, cushion, cyber, domain] vs WIDAI-only terms [adversarial, ai, algorithmic, bias, data, failure] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.857 (P=0.858, R=0.856). Balanced P/R — the 20-word DCWF element and 24-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (88.0%). "Knowledge of criticality and vulnerability factors (e.g., value, recuperation, c" and "Knowledge of AI risk taxonomy, including model risk, algorithmic bias, opacity," are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.519 > STS 0.455 (gap +0.064). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [knowledge, vulnerability] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 10.8%. Shared tokens: [knowledge, vulnerability]. DCWF-only: [applicability, countermeasures, criticality, cushion, cyber, domain]. WIDAI-only: [adversarial, ai, algorithmic, bias, data, failure].
